economic_notes: First Nation receives 92% of revenue from Government of Canada. COVID-19 impacted operations. Holds investment in Wasaya Partnership (modified equity). Guarantor for Happy Time Tours & Travel borrowings. Guaranteed up to $130,000 to suppliers of Nibinamik Community Store.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for fiscal year 2021, signed March 8, 2024 (filed very late - nearly 3 years after year-end). Qualifications relate to: (1) inability to observe physical inventories of $850,900 due to COVID quarantine measures; (2) insufficient audit evidence for investments in First Nation partnerships ($344,445 carrying value) and EIC portfolio investment ($182,721); (3) insufficient audit evidence for Social Services program expenses of $851,722. First Nation and Inuit Health previously reported separately in 2020 ($4,110,895) but appears consolidated into ISC in 2021. Note 20 shows a Ministry of Health discrepancy: total shown as 556,345 vs 566,345 in income statement. Non-compliance with First Nations Transparency Act filing deadline noted. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Nibinamik Power Authority and Nibinamik Education Authority. One GBE: Wasaya Partnership (modified equity method).
